_attribution: LA FORJA team (Manuel Peña Zúñiga). Original item authored by the team.
stem: In a bakery, the probability that any given customer buys bread is 0.7, independently of the other customers. Three customers walk in. What is the probability that they buy bread?
author_rationale: The author had 'all three buy bread' in mind: 0.7 · 0.7 · 0.7 = 0.343.

intended_defect.description: The phrase 'that they buy bread' leaves the quantifier unstated: it can be read as 'that all three buy bread' or as 'that at least one of them buys bread'. Both readings are reasonable for the plural subject 'three customers', and the stem does not choose between them.
intended_defect.expected_finding: Reading A = all three buy → 0.7³ = 0.343. Reading B = at least one buys → 1 - 0.3³ = 1 - 0.027 = 0.973. The answers differ, so the ambiguity attack is valid.
